evidence.snippet: On-target software verification for critical embedded systems

Rapita Systems provides on-target software verification tools and services globally to the embedded aerospace and automotive electronics industries. Our solutions help to increase software quality, deliver evidence to meet safety and certification objectives and reduce project costs.

items.notes: Software verification tools (code coverage, timing analysis, functional testing) and engineering services for DO-178C/ISO 26262-certified embedded systems including UAS avionics. RTBx is an on-target data logging hardware accessory.

value.address: Atlas House, Osbaldwick Link Road, York YO10 3JB

**Rapita Systems Ltd.**
Atlas House

UK

Rapita Systems provides on-target software verification tools and services globally to the embedded aerospace and automotive electronics industries. Our solutions help to increase software quality, deliver evidence to meet safety and certification objectives and reduce project costs.

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: Sells software verification tools to aerospace/UAS OEMs for DO-178C certification. Dedicated 'Certifying Unmanned Aircraft Systems' page; attending XPONENTIAL 2026. No drone hardware manufacturing (RTBx data logger is secondary).

items.name: Rapita Verification Suite (RVS)

items.name: RapiTest
items.product_type: functional testing tool for critical software

evidence.snippet: Rapi**Test** is the leading tool for functional testing of critical software.
Rapi**Cover** is the leading tool for structural code coverage analysis of critical software.

readiness.notes: Commercially released, versioned software products with global distribution and major aerospace customers (Collins Aerospace, BAE Systems, Embraer, Airbus).

tagline: Develops software verification and testing tools (Rapita Verification Suite, RapiTest, RapiCover, MACH178) for safety-critical embedded systems in aerospace and automotive; primary focus on DO-178C and ISO 26262 compliance. UK-based, production-ready. NDAA compliance and Blue UAS status unknown; public evidence does not indicate direct UAS platform contracts, though company positions tools for unmanned-systems certification workflows.
